name: Asparagus Crepes with Mushroom Dill-Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 small Onion, quartered and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crepes: Combine flour and salt in a mixing bowl. Make a well in the center and pour in the soymilk and oil. Beat until smooth.
2. Heat a 6 or 7-inch skillet. When hot, pour in about 1/4 cup of batter and tilt the skillet to coat evenly. Cook over moderate heat until lightly browned on the bottom.
3. Flip and brown the other side. Remove and set on a plate. Repeat with the remaining batter, resulting in 6 crepes.
4. For the sauce: Heat margarine in a small pot. Add onion and garlic and saute over moderate heat until golden.
5. Add mushrooms and cover. Cook until the mushrooms are limp and juicy. Sprinkle in flour and stir until it disappears.
6. Slowly pour in soymilk, stirring continuously. Bring to a simmer, then stir in dill and tarragon.
7. Cook at a simmer until the sauce thickens. Stir in lemon juice and season with salt and pepper to taste. Remove from heat and cover.
8. For filling: Trim about 1/2-inch of the asparagus stalks and scrape off any tough skin. Cut the stalks in half and steam until tender crisp.
9. Assemble: Place 6 asparagus stalks in the center of each crepe, letting the tips protrude from the top and overlapping the halved stalks if necessary.
10. Spoon a small amount of sauce over the asparagus. Fold one end of the crepe towards the center and overlap the other end over it.
11. Arrange the crepes, folded side down, in an oiled shallow baking dish. Spoon the remaining sauce evenly over the crepes.
12. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven until just heated through. Serve immediately.
